loot: The bioluminescent caps pulse gently as you reach between them.
One of the fungi has crystallised at its base. Something glints inside.
hazard: You brush a cap and it bursts, spraying acrid spores into your face.
The cluster flares bright. The light burns — it's not just light.
intel: The glow shifts in waves, rhythmic, pointing toward a nearby passage.
In the soft light you notice drag marks on the stone floor, leading somewhere.
flavor: The fungi hum faintly, a sound felt more than heard. Then silence.
Pale light plays across your hands. Beautiful, but useless.
The cluster seems to lean toward you, curious. It offers nothing.

loot: You press your palm to the largest cap. The mycelium network responds, pushing something to the surface.
The fungi recognize your gift. The ground splits and offers what it has been holding.
intel: Through the root network, you sense a pulse — something living, waiting, in a nearby chamber.
The mycelium whispers warnings. Something dangerous stirs close by.
flavor: The network acknowledges you. A warmth spreads through your hand, but nothing more.
You feel the vast web beneath the floor. It is ancient, and it is tired.

loot: Between eruptions you spot something wedged in the vent opening.
A previous explorer wasn't so lucky. Their pack lies half-buried in spore residue.
hazard: The vent erupts just as you lean in. Choking clouds engulf you.
A geyser of burning spores catches you full in the chest.
The vent hisses. You pull back — not fast enough.
intel: The spore plumes drift in one direction, carried by air from a nearby opening.
You notice the vent cycles in rhythm with something deeper in the caves.
flavor: The vent exhales softly, like a sleeping animal. You leave it be.
Spores drift lazily upward, catching faint light. Nothing here for you.

loot: You trace the pressure channels. There — a natural pocket, sealed by mineral buildup. You crack it open.
Understanding the eruption cycle, you time your reach perfectly into the vent shaft.
intel: The air pressure differential tells you there's a larger cavity connected nearby.
You calculate the spore dispersal pattern. It reveals the tunnel layout beyond this room.
flavor: Fascinating engineering — if it were engineered. The geology is remarkable but reveals nothing useful.
You map the vent's internal structure. Impressive, but practically useless right now.

loot: Beneath the fungal growth, the body still clutches something in its stiff fingers.
You peel back layers of mycelium. A belt pouch, still sealed against the damp.
hazard: As you disturb the body, something in the fungal mass contracts and bites.
The remains collapse inward. A cloud of decay-spores blooms from the cavity.
intel: The corpse points toward a passage, one arm outstretched. Running from something, or toward it.
flavor: Whoever this was, the cavern has claimed them completely. No face. No name. Just growth.
The remains are old. Older than you expected. How long has this place been hungry?
Fungal threads have woven through the bones like embroidery. The cave makes art of death.

loot: As you speak the words, the fungal growth recedes, releasing what it consumed. A final gift from the dead.
The remains settle peacefully. Something falls free — the cave gives back what it took.
intel: In the quiet after the rite, impressions flood in — this person's last moments, what they saw.
The departed's final memory surfaces: a warning about what lies in the next chamber.
flavor: You speak the old words. The air grows still. There is no answer, but the tension eases.
The rite is done. You hope it mattered to someone.

loot: The stump's interior is larger than it looks. Someone has been using it as a cache.
You reach into the hollow and feel leather — a pack, stashed and forgotten.
hazard: Something inside the stump moves. Fast. It bites before you can pull your hand back.
The wood is rotten. Your arm plunges through and something sharp catches your forearm.
intel: Scratch marks inside the hollow spell out crude directions. A previous explorer's trail.
Peering into the hollow, you see it connects to a gap beneath the floor.
flavor: The hollow is deep and dark and empty. Wind murmurs through it like a flute.
Old bark crumbles at your touch. Whatever this tree was, it died long before the cave took it.
The stump smells of damp earth and old wood. A familiar scent in an unfamiliar place.

loot: Your hands find what eyes can't. A hidden compartment, carved into the heartwood.
In the deepest shadow of the hollow, your fingers trace a seam — a concealed pocket.
intel: The shadows inside the stump connect to others across the room. You read the dark like a map.
From within the hollow, you hear air moving — there are passages nearby not shown on any map.
secret: The darkness inside is deeper than the stump should allow. There's a passage hidden here.
Your shadow-sense tingles. This stump connects to somewhere that shouldn't exist.
flavor: You melt into the shadow of the stump. Nothing here, but the darkness is comfortable.
The shadows welcome you but offer nothing. Sometimes darkness is just darkness.

loot: The liquid pooling beneath the cap has crystallised into something valuable.
Droplets have eroded a small hollow in the stone below. Something shiny sits in the pool.
hazard: A fat drop lands on your hand. It burns — acidic, not water.
You taste the liquid. Mistake. Your mouth goes numb immediately.
The cap shudders at your touch and weeps caustic sap down your arm.
flavor: Drip. Drip. Drip. The cap sweats endlessly. It means nothing.
The liquid is faintly sweet-smelling. Probably poisonous. You leave it alone.
A fat mushroom, weeping in the dark. You feel a strange kinship.

loot: You shake the cap roughly. Something dislodges from its underside and clatters to the ground.
The cap tears free and rolls aside, revealing a small stash beneath.
hazard: The cap ruptures, drenching you in foul liquid that burns on contact.
You grab the cap. Bad idea — the underside is lined with barbed filaments.
The whole fungus collapses in a wet, toxic heap. You breathe in something awful.
flavor: You kick the cap over. It flops wetly. Nothing underneath but damp stone.
The cap bounces once and lies still. The cave doesn't care about your vandalism.

loot: The silk is impossibly fine. You peel back a layer and find something wrapped inside, preserved.
Whatever made this cocoon was wrapping something for later. Finder's keepers.
hazard: The cocoon twitches. Something inside is not dead.
You pull a thread and the whole sac convulses. Tiny legs pour from the opening.
Sticky filaments cling to your hands. The more you pull, the tighter they grip.
intel: The cocoon contains husks — several small creatures, drained. Whatever hunts here is close.
flavor: The silk sways gently in a breeze you can't feel. Beautiful and deeply unsettling.
You stare at the cocoon. It does not stare back. Probably.
Dense white silk, carefully wound. The architect is absent. For now.

loot: Your blade parts the silk cleanly. Inside — supplies, wrapped and preserved by the silk's properties.
One clean cut. The cocoon spills its contents: something useful, wrapped in something dead.
hazard: You slash the sac open. The occupant was alive, and angry.
The blade cuts true but the tension in the silk snaps it back like a whip across your arm.
intel: Inside the cocoon you find the remains of a scout — and their annotated map.
Cut open, the sac reveals markings on the wall behind it, hidden from casual view.
flavor: Your blade hisses through the silk. Nothing inside but dust and old chitin.
You cut it open because you could. It contained nothing. Not everything does.

loot: The water is shallow. Your hand touches something solid on the bottom — not stone.
Coins glint beneath the surface film. An offering, or a trap? Either way, they're yours now.
hazard: The water is warm. Too warm. Your skin prickles where it touched.
Something in the pool wraps around your wrist. Thin, strong, and pulling downward.
intel: The pool's surface ripples in one direction. Underground current — another passage feeds this.
In the still water you see a reflection that doesn't match the ceiling. An illusion, or a warning.
flavor: The pool is glass-still and utterly dark. Your reflection stares back, looking tired.
You cup the water. It tastes of iron and old stone. Drinkable, but barely.
Tiny translucent creatures swim in lazy circles. They ignore you completely.

loot: The mineral composition is unusual — the sediment contains refined material. Not natural. You extract it.
Your analysis reveals trace metals in the silt. You filter the pool and recover something valuable.
intel: The water chemistry tells a story: this pool is fed by runoff from a larger chamber nearby.
Dissolved compounds in the water match those found near active creature nests. Be careful ahead.
The mineral signature is distinct. You can trace it to map the water table — and the rooms above.
flavor: Clean water, mild mineral content, no toxins. Scientifically uninteresting.
The sample tells you this pool has been here for centuries. That's all it tells you.

loot: One of the puffballs has grown around something. You peel it apart carefully.
The ring has formed over an old campsite. A buried pouch peeks through the soil.
hazard: You step into the ring. Every puffball detonates simultaneously. You can't breathe.
The ring is a trap — pressure-sensitive. Spores erupt in a choking wall.
intel: The ring is a perfect circle. This is territorial marking. Something claims this room.
flavor: The puffballs sit in perfect formation, soft and round and completely pointless.
You crouch beside the ring. The fungi are arranged with geometric precision. Nature is showing off.
A fairy ring. In a cave. Without fairies. Just damp and silence.
You resist the urge to kick one. Barely.

loot: You stomp through the ring. Under the last puffball, something solid — a small cache.
Beneath the scattered remains of the ring, the soil is loose. Something's buried here.
hazard: You punt a puffball. It detonates on impact. Then the next one goes. Chain reaction.
The ring erupts in a cascade of burning spores. Your eyes stream and your lungs seize.
You break the ring and the ground trembles. Something below takes offense.
flavor: You scatter the puffballs. They roll gently across the floor. The cave judges you silently.
The ring is broken. Nothing happens. You feel vaguely guilty.

loot: The roots have grown around something — crushed but intact. You work it free.
Deep in the tangle, wrapped in rootlets like a gift, something catches the light.
hazard: The roots constrict around your arm as you reach in. Living wood, and it's strong.
You disturb a nest woven into the roots. The inhabitants object violently.
intel: The roots trace a path along the wall and disappear into a crack leading deeper.
Following the root system with your eyes, you see it spans toward an adjacent chamber.
flavor: Roots from a surface world you can barely remember. They've come a long way down.
The tangle pulses faintly. Growing, always growing, with nowhere left to go.
Gnarled wood and pale rootlets, knotted like arthritic fingers. Nothing to find here.

loot: Your deft fingers work the knots apart. There — wedged in the core, something wrapped in oilcloth.
You read the tangle like a puzzle. The solution reveals a hidden cache at its centre.
intel: As you work the roots apart, you uncover scratch marks on the wall behind — a crude map.
The tangle was placed here deliberately, concealing markings. Someone wanted these found by the right person.
secret: The roots hide a gap in the wall — narrow, but passable. It leads somewhere unmapped.
Behind the tangle, a passage. The roots grew to conceal it, or were planted to.
flavor: You untangle the roots methodically. There's nothing inside but more roots.
The knots come apart easily in your hands. A satisfying puzzle with no prize.

loot: The face's mouth is a knot-hole. Inside it, tucked like a secret, something small and hard.
One of the eyes is a polished stone, worked smooth. It comes free with a twist.
hazard: The face weeps sap as you touch it. The sap burns where it lands on your skin.
intel: The face looks toward one of the exits. Coincidence, or a warning?
There are more faces like this one — you just didn't see them before. They all face the same direction.
flavor: The face watches you with knothole eyes. It has been watching for a very long time.
You stare at the face. It stares back. Neither of you blinks.
Who carved this? Or did the wood simply grow tired of being featureless?
The expression could be anything. Grief. Amusement. Hunger. You decide not to decide.

loot: You bow your head and speak softly. The face's mouth opens — not carved, but moving — and something drops out.
Your prayer is answered. The bark splits and offers a small blessing.
intel: The face speaks in impressions, not words. Danger ahead. Something that shouldn't be disturbed.
Through your prayer, the face shares a vision: the layout of nearby chambers, seen from root-level.
flavor: The face seems to soften as you pray. A trick of the light, surely.
You feel acknowledged. Not answered — just acknowledged. Perhaps that's enough.
The prayer echoes strangely. The face seems satisfied, though nothing has changed.

loot: The crate's contents have mostly decayed, but one item survived the damp.
Between rotting planks you find a wrapped bundle, still dry inside its oilskin.
Most of the crate is mush. But at the bottom, something solid.
hazard: The wood gives way and your hand plunges into something sharp inside — rusted nails.
The crate collapses. A colony of stinging insects boils out of the wreckage.
intel: Supply markings on the crate — someone has been provisioning routes through here.
flavor: Rotten wood, rotten contents, rotten luck. Nothing salvageable.
Who hauls a crate this deep underground? And what happened to them?

loot: The lock is rusted but the mechanism still works. Inside: the good stuff, sealed away from the rot.
Click. The lock yields. Whatever was worth locking away is still here.
The locked compartment is bone-dry inside. Someone packed this to last.
intel: The lock is trapped — but you spotted it. Inside, a journal. Supply routes, guard schedules.
The locked section contains correspondence. Someone was planning something in these caves.
flavor: You pick the lock with a satisfying click. Inside: empty. Someone beat you to it.
The locked compartment held something once. The impression in the velvet lining suggests a ring.

loot: The crystal is loose in its setting. It comes free with a firm twist.
Light refracts through the crystal. At this angle you can see something embedded inside.
hazard: The crystal is sharp. Sharper than it looks. Blood wells before you feel the cut.
You touch the crystal and static arcs through your hand. The charge is vicious.
The node shatters at your touch. Crystal shards pepper your face.
intel: The crystal vibrates at a frequency you can feel in your teeth. Something is generating power nearby.
flavor: The crystal pulses with a soft internal light. It means nothing you can decipher.
Beautiful. Useless. The eternal combination of things found underground.
You press your thumb against the facets. It's warm. Crystals shouldn't be warm.

loot: You wedge your blade behind the crystal and lever it free. It's heavier than it looks — and valuable.
Brute force works. The crystal pops from the rock face trailing sparks.
hazard: The crystal resists, then gives suddenly. The backlash sends crystal splinters into your forearm.
You wrench it free and the stone around it fractures. A shard of rock catches your shoulder.
intel: Behind the crystal, the wall is hollow. You can hear movement on the other side.
flavor: You pry the crystal loose. In your hands, it stops glowing. Just a rock now.
It comes free easily — too easily. This one is depleted, whatever it once held.

loot: The burrow's occupant is a hoarder. Shiny things line the nest — one of them is useful.
A small creature scurries away as you peer in. It left something behind.
hazard: Something in the burrow doesn't appreciate visitors. Teeth, small but numerous.
You reach in blind. The occupant is home, and territorial.
The burrow isn't empty. The thing inside is fast, and it aims for the eyes.
intel: The burrow has multiple exits. This animal knows routes through the cave that you don't.
Claw marks around the burrow entrance lead toward a passage — a well-worn trail.
flavor: A small, warm hole in the cold stone. Something lives here, curled and sleeping. You leave it be.
Tiny bones and seed husks. Life persists, even here.
The burrow smells of animal musk and old moss. Cozy, for a hole in the ground.

loot: You rig a small light and thread it into the tunnel. The burrow connects to a cache — supplies, stashed and forgotten.
Your careful examination of the tunnel network reveals a hidden compartment, partially collapsed.
intel: The tunnel network maps onto the room layout perfectly. You can predict what's in adjacent chambers.
Disturbed soil, fresh claw marks — the local fauna is agitated. Something's changed recently.
Air currents through the burrow tell you about connected spaces. Your mental map expands.
flavor: The burrow system is impressively engineered for something without thumbs. But it tells you nothing new.
You map the tunnel network thoroughly. It leads to more tunnels. That's it.

loot: The carvings frame a hollow in the stump's crown. Inside — an offering, long uncollected.
You trace the carvings. One symbol is a latch. The stump opens like a reliquary.
hazard: The carvings are warded. Your fingers tingle, then burn, as old protections flare.
You touch the wrong symbol. The stump pulses and something strikes you from within.
intel: The carvings tell a story — a map, disguised as mythology. You recognise landmarks.
The altar faces a specific direction. Following the sight line reveals a hidden mark on the far wall.
secret: The carvings describe a hidden chamber. The directions are encoded, but readable.
One carving depicts a door. Not metaphorical — there's a passage here, behind the altar.
flavor: The carvings are ancient, worn smooth by countless touching hands. You add yours to the count.
An altar to something you don't recognise. The old gods of the underground, perhaps.
The stump hums faintly when you place your hands on it. Just vibration. Just stone and wood.

loot: You sanctify the altar. The carvings glow and the hollow opens fully. Inside — a sacred offering, preserved by old magic.
Your consecration unlocks something. The stump splits along ancient seams, revealing its heart.
intel: The altar responds to your consecration with visions — brief, violent impressions of what lies ahead.
The sanctified altar pulses once. You know, suddenly, where the greatest danger waits.
secret: The consecrated altar reveals what was hidden: a seam in the floor, a passage descending.
Your blessing awakens the altar fully. It shows you a door that wasn't there before.
flavor: The consecration takes. A sense of peace settles over the stump. Nothing else changes.
You complete the rite. The altar is at rest now. Whatever haunted it is gone.

loot: Something is tangled in the vines — caught on its way through, perhaps. Still useful.
A vine has grown around a small object, holding it suspended at eye level.
hazard: The vines are covered in fine barbs you didn't see. Your hands come away bleeding.
You part the curtain and something drops from above — heavy, sharp, and aimed at your head.
intel: Through the vines you can see the wall behind them — marked with directions, arrow-scratched.
The vines conceal an alcove with a clear view into the next passage.
secret: Behind the curtain: not wall, but open space. A passage, hidden by the growth.
The vines conceal an opening. Cool air flows through it — another chamber beyond.
flavor: The vines sway as you pass. There's no breeze. They moved on their own.
A living curtain, green and grey, dividing nothing from nothing.
You part the vines. Behind them: a wall. Just a wall. The vines fall back into place.

loot: You force your way through. The vines resist, then give. On the other side, you find a supply stash.
Pushing through the curtain, you stumble into a small alcove. Someone's abandoned pack sits here.
hazard: The vines wrap around your limbs as you push. They tighten. You tear free, but not unscathed.
You push through and fall — the floor drops away on the other side. A short tumble, but painful.
secret: The curtain hides a narrow passage. It shouldn't be here — it's not on any map.
You push through into a space that shouldn't exist. The cave has secrets behind its curtains.
flavor: You push through the curtain. It's just more cave on the other side. Anticlimactic.
The vines cling and grab but there's nothing beyond them. You retreat, covered in sap.

loot: The sack is torn but not empty. Someone left in a hurry.
You rummage through mouldy cloth and find something worth keeping.
hazard: A centipede the length of your forearm curls out of the sack and strikes.
The sack is full of broken glass. You discover this the hard way.
flavor: Rotten food, torn fabric, nothing useful. A sorry end to someone's expedition.
Empty wrappers and a cracked waterskin. Whoever left this took what mattered.

loot: You tear the sack apart. At the very bottom, wrapped in oilcloth — something intact.
Aggressive searching pays off. A sealed pouch tumbles out.
hazard: You shove your hand deep and something bites. Hard.
The sack was sitting on a nest. The occupants swarm up your arms.
flavor: You shake out every last crumb. Nothing. Just dust and disappointment.
Thorough destruction of an empty sack. Very efficient.

loot: The torch was used as a makeshift holder. Something is lashed to the handle.
Wrapped around the base — a small bundle, hidden by the soot.
intel: The torch points the way its owner was heading. Drag marks on the floor confirm the direction.
Soot marks on the walls trace a path. Someone was marking their route.
flavor: Cold and spent. Whoever carried this is long gone.
The pitch has hardened. This torch burned its last hours ago. Or years.
A simple pine torch. The upper caves don't warrant anything fancier.

loot: The crack is wide enough to reach into. Your fingers close around something solid.
Wedged in the fissure — a small pouch, pressed flat by the shifting stone.
hazard: Loose rubble shifts as you probe the crack. A chunk of ceiling clips your shoulder.
Something in the crack bites your finger. You can't see what it was.
intel: Air moves through the crack. There's open space on the other side.
You press your ear to the crack. Faint sounds — movement in an adjacent passage.
secret: The crack runs floor to ceiling. With effort, it might widen into a passage.
Behind the crack, a hollow. It's not natural — someone sealed this opening.
flavor: Just a crack. The mountain settling, nothing more.
You peer through the fissure. Darkness, and more darkness. Riveting.

loot: You hammer the crack wider. Behind the stone — a sealed niche with something inside.
Brute force reveals a hidden cavity. The builders didn't want this found.
hazard: The wall gives way faster than expected. Rubble cascades onto you.
You break through, but the collapse sends shards of stone into your arms.
secret: The crack becomes a gap, then a passage. Somewhere nobody's walked in a long time.
You force the wall apart. Behind it — a tunnel, hand-carved, leading into darkness.
flavor: You widen the crack to find... more wall. Solid rock behind it.
A lot of effort for a slightly larger crack. The mountain wins this round.

loot: The anchor holds a frayed rope. At the end, still attached — a climber's pack.
Someone left gear clipped to the anchor. Their loss, your gain.
intel: The rope leads down a shaft. The knots suggest experienced climbers came this way.
Marks around the anchor tell a story — multiple expeditions, different years.
flavor: A piton driven deep into the rock. The rope is long gone.
Professional work. Whoever set this knew what they were doing. Didn't save them.
The anchor is solid. The rope that hung from it was not.

loot: The rats have been collecting. Among the debris — something that glints.
A magpie instinct. The nest is full of stolen trinkets, one of which is useful.
hazard: The rats are home. And they don't share.
You disturb the nest and a dozen furious rodents pour out, biting everything.
The mother rat is the size of a cat. She disagrees with your presence.
intel: The rats have chewed through a wall in the back of the nest. A tunnel leads somewhere.
Rat droppings trail toward a passage you hadn't noticed.
flavor: Shredded cloth, gnawed bones, and the smell. The smell is the worst part.
A nest of ordinary rats in an ordinary cave. The mundanity is almost comforting.
The rats watch you from the shadows with bead-black eyes. Judging.

loot: You catalog the rats' hoard systematically. Most is junk — but not all.
Tracking the rats' foraging routes, you find their best stash.
intel: The rats have mapped this cave system better than you. Their trails reveal the layout.
Droppings, claw marks, fur traces — the rats show you where everything connects.
You follow the rats' paths. They always know where the dangers are. And aren't.
flavor: You study the nest with scientific detachment. Conclusion: it's a rat nest.
Thorough analysis confirms the rats are just... rats. Disappointing.

loot: The markings are a crude map. An X marks a loose stone nearby — and something behind it.
intel: Tally marks, arrows, warnings. A previous explorer left notes for whoever came next.
The scratches form a simple map. Not complete, but enough to warn you about something ahead.
Numbers scratched beside arrows. Someone was counting rooms. Their count stops abruptly.
flavor: Names. Dates. The usual graffiti of the desperate. None of them came back.
"TURN BACK" in shaky letters. Solid advice, poorly taken.
Scratches too crude to read. Could be words, could be claw marks. Could be both.

loot: The beam crushed a shelf when it fell. Among the wreckage — something intact.
Under the beam, pinned flat, a pack that still has contents.
hazard: You shift the beam and the ceiling groans. Dust and small stones rain down.
The beam rolls as you investigate. It catches your shin before you can jump clear.
intel: The beam was cut, not broken. Someone brought this ceiling down deliberately.
Marks on the beam suggest it was load-bearing. The collapse blocked a passage.
flavor: Old timber, dry-rotted and splintered. This mine hasn't been maintained in decades.
The beam creaks when you touch it. Best left alone.
Evidence of old industry. Someone worked these caves once. Now the caves work on their own.

loot: You heave the beam aside. Beneath it — a crushed crate, but its contents survived.
The beam shifts with effort. It was covering a supply cache, maybe intentionally.
hazard: You lift the beam and the ceiling above shifts ominously. Stones strike your back.
The beam is heavier than it looks. Something in your shoulder gives before the wood does.
secret: The beam was blocking a passage. Not collapsed over it — placed across it.
You move the beam and find a trapdoor beneath. Someone didn't want this found.
flavor: You lift the beam. Underneath: dirt, stones, disappointment.
Impressive show of strength. Zero practical results. The beam goes back down.

loot: The mud has swallowed something. You fish it out — still usable.
Light catches something under the murky surface. Worth grabbing.
hazard: The puddle is deeper than it looks. Your foot plunges through into a hidden crevice.
The mud conceals sharp debris. You step on something unpleasant.
intel: Fresh footprints in the mud, not yours. Something passed through recently.
The puddle is fed by a seep from the wall. Water flows from somewhere nearby.
flavor: Mud, water, probably rat urine. The glamour of exploration.
Your reflection stares up at you from the brown water. You both look tired.
A puddle. In a cave. Truly, the wonders never cease.

loot: The lantern is broken but the fuel reservoir is intact. And there's something stuffed inside.
Behind the cracked glass, a small object wrapped in cloth. Hidden deliberately.
hazard: You cut yourself on the broken glass. Deeper than expected.
Residual oil ignites when you jostle the lantern. Brief but painful.
intel: The lantern is military issue. Soldiers came this way — and didn't come back.
An inscription on the base — initials and a date. Recent.
flavor: A tin lantern, dented and dark. Its light-giving days are over.
The glass is cracked in a starburst pattern. Impact, not age. Thrown or dropped.
Oil stains around the base. Someone sat here a while. Then left.

loot: You disassemble the lantern efficiently. The reflector plate is salvageable — and behind it, a hidden compartment.
The mechanism contains more than oil. A tiny cache, built into the frame.
intel: The lantern's design tells you who made it and when. This is recent expedition gear.
Residue analysis — the oil is local, refined from cave fauna. Someone was living down here.
flavor: Excellent craftsmanship for a broken lamp. You admire it. It remains broken.
You understand exactly how this lantern works. That knowledge helps no one.

loot: One geode has already cracked. Inside, a crystal worth keeping — and something else.
The cluster contains a hollow geode. Inside it, wrapped in mineral deposits, something useful.
hazard: The crystal shatters under your touch. Shards fly — one catches your cheek.
The geode is pressurised. It bursts, spraying needle-fine crystal fragments.
intel: The crystal growth patterns point consistently in one direction. Following them might lead somewhere.
Resonance from the cluster — it vibrates in sympathy with something deeper in the caves.
flavor: Beautiful amethyst formations, worthless without a jeweller. You admire and move on.
The geodes hum faintly. Harmonic resonance, nothing magical. Still eerie.
Light fragments into rainbows across the cave wall. The crystal doesn't care about your quest.

loot: You crack the geode open with controlled force. The prize inside is intact.
One clean strike splits the cluster. Something valuable tumbles out of the core.
hazard: The crystal explodes outward when freed. You shield your face but your arms take the worst.
The cluster was under tension. It snaps and a shard embeds in your hand.
flavor: You break it open. Pretty crystals, nothing more. Geology is not treasure.
The geode cracks neatly in half. Both halves are empty. Perfectly, mockingly empty.

loot: The spire's base has eroded, revealing something embedded in the crystal matrix.
A previous visitor hung something from the spire's tip. Still there, swaying gently.
hazard: The spire's frequency shifts as you approach. The sound becomes painful, then physical.
You touch the spire and it screams. The vibration travels through your bones.
The harmonic shatters a nearby formation. Crystal shrapnel peppers your side.
intel: The spire resonates with others in adjacent chambers. You can hear the layout in the harmonics.
The singing changes pitch near certain walls — thinner stone, possible passages.
flavor: The spire sings a single, unwavering note. It has been singing for centuries.
You stand in the sound and feel it in your chest. Beautiful and deeply lonely.
The crystal hums like a tuning fork struck by god. You have nothing to tune.

loot: You map the resonance nodes. One is a stress point — tap it precisely and a crystal shard drops free, flawless.
Frequency analysis reveals the spire is growing around an object. You extract it without damage.
intel: The harmonics encode the cave structure. You can map three chambers without entering them.
Resonance patterns reveal the crystal network extends far beyond this room. You trace the connections.
The singing frequency matches seismic stress patterns. You can predict where passages are weakest.
flavor: The acoustic properties are remarkable. You document everything. It helps no one here.
You understand the physics perfectly. The spire is unimpressed by your understanding.

loot: The water is so clear you can see the bottom. Something rests there, crystallised in mineral deposits.
Light refracting through the pool reveals an object on the far edge, half-submerged.
hazard: You reach in and the water burns cold. Mineral-saturated and corrosive.
The pool reflects light into your eyes. Blinding. You stumble and cut yourself on a crystal edge.
intel: The pool reflects the ceiling perfectly. In the reflection, you see a passage you missed looking up.
Ripples move against the current. Something feeds this pool from an unseen source.
flavor: Your reflection is clearer than any mirror. You look worse than you thought.
The pool is glass-still. A perfect circle of liquid crystal. You dare not disturb it.
Light plays through the water and casts prismatic patterns on the ceiling. Nature's chandelier.

loot: You touch the water and speak quietly. The pool responds — the surface parts, revealing something on the bottom.
Your communion clarifies the water. Objects on the bottom become visible and retrievable.
intel: The pool shows you visions — brief, fractured images of nearby chambers and their dangers.
Through the water, you see echoes of those who came before. Their path becomes yours.
The pool becomes a scrying surface. You see what lies ahead, dimly but clearly enough.
flavor: The water warms at your touch. A blessing accepted, but nothing offered in return.
You commune with the stillness. The pool has been here longer than anything alive. It has patience.

loot: The fracture runs through the pillar's core. Inside the crack — something crystallised around an object.
The pillar split around a foreign body. Whatever was embedded in it is now accessible.
hazard: You touch the fracture line and the pillar shifts. A slab of crystal shears off toward you.
The pillar is unstable. Your examination tips it further and debris rains from above.
intel: The fracture pattern is deliberate. Someone cracked this pillar to mark a trail.
The pillar broke along a seam. Natural crystal doesn't fracture this way — force was applied.
flavor: A once-magnificent crystal column, now split and leaning. Time wins every argument.
The fracture catches light and splits it into a permanent rainbow. A memorial to structural failure.
You push against the pillar. It doesn't budge. Whatever cracked it was stronger than you.

loot: Your fingers find seams invisible to others. A hidden cache, tucked into the fracture.
The crack system is a maze. Your hands navigate it by touch and instinct.
intel: The fractures tell a story of stress and impact. You can read the cave's trauma like a book.
Through the cracks, air moves. You trace the draft to an unmapped passage.
secret: The pillar is hollow. The fracture is a door, if you know how to open it.
You find a cavity behind the fracture, large enough to crawl into. It leads somewhere.
flavor: You explore every crack with expert fingers. Nothing but crystal dust and cold air.
The shadows inside the fractures are absolute. Even you find nothing in them.

loot: The prism has grown around something. You can see it inside, perfectly preserved.
The shard is loose. Behind it, tucked into the wall — a small stash.
hazard: The shard focuses ambient light into a beam. It burns where it touches your hand.
You grasp the prism and it splinters. Crystal needles drive into your palm.
flavor: Rainbow light dances across your face. Pretty but pointless.
The shard is perfectly formed. A natural prism, splitting light endlessly.
You hold the prism up to your eye. The world shatters into colour. Then you put it down.

loot: The shard snaps cleanly. Behind it, pressed into the wall socket — something useful.
You wrench the prism free. It shatters, but reveals a cache behind it.
hazard: The shard detonates when you snap it. Crystal shrapnel everywhere.
You break the shard and focused light lances through the gap. It scorches your forearm.
The prism was load-bearing, somehow. The wall above shifts and stones tumble down.
flavor: You break a perfectly good prism. No treasure. No secret. Just broken crystal.
The shard snaps and the light patterns die. The cave is dimmer now. Well done.

loot: The crystal hasn't reached the figure's outstretched hand. It still holds something.
The encasement is thinner at the base. You can reach the figure's belt pouch.
hazard: The crystal around the figure is unstable. A piece breaks free and strikes you.
As you lean close, the figure's hand twitches. Reflex or... alive? Either way, it hurts.
intel: The figure's gear marks them as a scout. Their map is visible through the crystal, readable if you squint.
The expression on the frozen face tells you everything about what they saw last.
flavor: A person, perfectly preserved in crystal. Their expression is peaceful. Or perhaps resigned.
The crystal grows slowly. This person has been here decades, maybe centuries. Still whole.
You look into the figure's eyes through the crystal. There's no one behind them anymore.

loot: Your prayer softens the crystal around the figure's hands. Something drops free.
As you speak the rite, the crystal recedes from the figure's pack. You can reach it now.
intel: The departed speaks through your prayer — fragmented memories of the route they took.
In death, they share what they knew. The path ahead becomes clearer.
flavor: You speak the words. The crystal seems to warm. Perhaps they hear. Perhaps not.
The rite echoes strangely off the crystal. For a moment, the figure seems to exhale.

loot: At the bottom of the well, among crystallised water droplets, something solid.
The light reveals objects embedded in the well's crystal walls. One is within reach.
hazard: The concentrated light burns your skin. This well focuses energy like a lens.
You lean over the well and vertigo hits. The light seems to pull you downward.
intel: The well channels light from above. Following its source reveals connected chambers.
Reflections in the well show passages at angles — you can see around corners through the crystal.
flavor: A shaft of pure light, reaching up through crystal into darkness. No source visible.
The well is warm. Life could grow here, in this single column of light. Nothing has.
You stand in the light and feel almost safe. Almost.

loot: The light responds to your communion. It intensifies, and something falls from the shaft above.
Your prayer is answered with warmth and a gift — crystallised within the light's focal point.
intel: The light shows you the way. Through it, you see the cave system as if from above.
Your communion opens a vision — the dungeon's structure laid bare for a flickering moment.
The light carries impressions. Danger, safety, secrets — you sense them all nearby.
flavor: The light embraces you. For a moment, the darkness doesn't exist. Then it returns.
You commune with the light. It has been shining alone for a very long time.

loot: The chest's lock has corroded away. Inside — waterlogged but salvageable goods.
Barnacles pry off in chunks. The chest opens reluctantly. Something dry remains inside.
The salt has preserved the contents better than you'd expect.
hazard: The barnacles are razor-sharp. Your hands are bleeding before you get the chest open.
Something is living inside the chest. It strikes when the lid opens.
flavor: Saltwater and rot. The chest's contents dissolved long ago.
The chest is fused shut by mineral deposits. Without tools, it's just a barnacled rock.

loot: You work the corroded mechanism with precision. The sealed compartment opens — dry inside.
The false bottom releases with a click. The real contents were hidden from the water.
Your fingers find the trick panel. Behind the barnacles — a dry cache.
intel: The chest contains correspondence, sealed in wax. Trade routes through the flooded passages.
Inside, a map wrapped in oilskin. The flooded passages are charted.
flavor: You open every compartment. All empty, all damp. Someone was thorough before you.
The mechanism is elegant work, ruined by salt. You appreciate the craft. The craft doesn't help.

loot: Something is tangled in the kelp, caught by the current.
The kelp has grown around a floating object. You untangle it carefully.
hazard: The kelp wraps your wrist and pulls. There's a current behind it, and it's strong.
You part the fronds and something lunges from behind them. An eel, and it bites.
intel: Water flows through the curtain from one direction. There's a larger chamber behind it.
The kelp grows thickest where nutrients flow. Following the growth pattern reveals the water's source.
secret: Behind the kelp — not a wall, but open water. A flooded passage, concealed.
The curtain hides a gap in the rock. The current flows through it.
flavor: Thick fronds of cave kelp, swaying in the dark water. Hypnotic and useless.
The kelp smells of brine and old life. The sea has no business being this far underground.

loot: You force through the curtain. On the other side, caught on a rock shelf — abandoned gear.
Beyond the kelp, a small air pocket and a dry ledge. Someone left supplies here.
hazard: The kelp tangles your legs as you push through. You go under briefly.
You push through and drop into deeper water. The current grabs you before you recover.
secret: Behind the curtain — a flooded passage, low-ceilinged but passable. It goes somewhere.
The kelp was deliberately cultivated to hide this opening. Someone didn't want it found.
flavor: You push through into more flooded cave. The kelp slides across your face. Unpleasant.
Nothing but wet stone and more kelp on the other side. The cave mocks your initiative.

loot: The statue's outstretched hands hold an offering bowl. There's still something in it.
The base has eroded, revealing a sealed compartment beneath the plinth.
hazard: The statue's stone is slick with algae. You slip and crack your elbow on the plinth.
You disturb the silt around the base. Something that was resting there isn't happy.
intel: The statue faces a specific direction. Its gaze leads to a passage partially hidden by water.
Inscriptions on the base, partially legible. Directions, encoded in prayer.
flavor: A stone figure, half-submerged, arms raised in supplication. The water doesn't care.
The statue's face has been worn smooth by centuries of water. No identity. No prayer answered.
Whoever this depicted, they're forgotten. The statue persists. The meaning doesn't.

loot: You kneel in the water and pray. The statue responds — the offering bowl tilts, releasing its contents to you.
Your prayer drains the water around the base. A sealed reliquary is exposed.
intel: The statue's empty eyes fill with light as you pray. Visions of the flooded passages, complete.
The drowned god listens. It shows you what the water hides.
Through prayer, the inscription becomes clear. A complete map of the sunken chambers.
flavor: You pray at the feet of a forgotten god. The water laps gently. Nothing else.
The statue seems warmer after your prayer. Imagination, probably. Probably.

loot: A dry shelf in the pocket holds a waterproof bag. Someone cached supplies here.
The air pocket extends to a small dry ledge. Something has been left here.
intel: The air comes from somewhere. Tracing the draft reveals connected passages above water level.
The pocket tells you the cave breathes. There are open spaces above the water line.
Bubbles rise from a crack in the floor. The air source is below — another chamber, dry.
flavor: You breathe the stale air gratefully. A dry ceiling in a wet world. Small mercies.
The pocket smells of trapped gas and old stone. Better than drowning.
A bubble of breathable air in a flooded nightmare. The cave's one concession to the living.

loot: Among the shells, something that isn't shell. Smooth, worked, valuable.
The mound contains a pearl. Not large, but real.
hazard: The shells are sharp as knives. You learn this while digging through them.
Something living hides in the mound. It pinches hard enough to draw blood.
You disturb the mound and a venomous cone shell strikes your finger.
intel: The shells are from deep-water species. The flood comes from the sea itself.
The mound is a midden — something intelligent piled these here deliberately.
flavor: Shells from creatures that shouldn't exist underground. The sea disagrees.
You pick through the shells. Spirals and whorls from species you can't name.
A cairn of sea-death in an underground lake. Nothing about this is natural.

loot: Systematic sorting reveals a pattern — and buried at the centre, the builder's prize.
You identify species distribution. The rarest shells cluster around something deliberate.
intel: The species composition tells you about water flow. You can map the flooded passages from the ecology.
Shell types change by depth and salinity. You can predict which chambers are fully submerged.
The mound is a territorial marker. Something claims this area — and its range extends three rooms.
flavor: Detailed shell taxonomy, exhaustively catalogued. Scientifically fascinating. Practically worthless.
You sort every shell by species. Results: a sorted pile of shells.

loot: Something is caught in the grate bars, washed there by the current.
The grate has trapped debris. Among the rubbish — something worth keeping.
hazard: The rusted bars slice your hands. Tetanus is the least of your worries down here.
You rattle the grate and the ceiling above rumbles. Stop that.
intel: Water flows through the grate from below. There's a whole system of flooded passages beneath you.
The grate is engineered. Someone built drainage here. The infrastructure implies habitation.
secret: One section of the grate swings open — a hatch, not just a drain cover.
The grate hides a passage. The rust makes it look permanent. It isn't.
flavor: Iron bars, red with corrosion, set into the floor. Whatever they kept in or out is long gone.
You peer through the grate into darkness and water. Nothing peers back. Probably.

loot: The grate comes free with a groan. Underneath — a waterlogged stash.
You bend the bars apart. Behind them, wedged into the drain — someone's emergency pack.
hazard: The grate fights you. A bar snaps and the jagged end rakes your forearm.
You lift the grate and the floor beneath gives way. A short, wet, painful fall.
secret: The grate opens onto a ladder. A submerged passage, hand-carved, leads somewhere.
Under the grate — not a drain but a shaft. The water is only knee-deep. You can pass.
flavor: You wrench the grate free. Underneath: more water, more stone. No reward for effort.
The grate was stuck by centuries of corrosion. Now it's unstuck. Nothing else changes.

loot: The bloom grows over something. You brush the tendrils aside and find a preserved artifact.
Light-producing organisms cluster around a mineral deposit. Part of it is extractable.
hazard: The bloom releases a burst of toxin when touched. Your hand swells immediately.
You disturb the colony. The organisms secrete acid in self-defense.
intel: The bloom traces water channels along the walls. You can read the flow patterns — and the layout.
Phosphorescence is brightest near air sources. You can find dry passages by following the light.
flavor: The water glows soft blue-green around the bloom. Peaceful. Almost worth the drowning risk.
Tiny organisms, painting the darkness with cold light. They ask nothing and give everything.
You cup the glowing water in your hands. It fades as it leaves the colony. Keep walking.

loot: Someone hid something inside a skull. Clever. You find it by weight.
The pile has been curated. Offerings sit between the skulls — one is still useful.
hazard: The pile shifts. A skull rolls and the rest follow — a small avalanche of bone and teeth.
You reach into the pile and something inside it grabs back. Bone fingers, still articulated.
intel: The skulls are marked. Different species, different ages. This is a trophy collection.
One skull has a map scratched into its cranium. Crude, but readable.
flavor: Hundreds of skulls, stacked with care. Someone counted them. You don't want to.
The empty sockets watch you from every angle. They've seen everyone who came before.
You pick up a skull. It's heavier than expected. Something rattles inside. You put it back.

loot: Your rite releases the tension in the pile. It settles peacefully, revealing a buried offering.
The dead relinquish their treasures gratefully. Something rolls free from the bottom of the pile.
intel: The dead speak in chorus. Fragments of memory — where they walked, where they fell.
Through the rite, you feel the history of each skull. The last thoughts converge on a warning.
The pile sighs. In the exhale, you understand the layout of these halls better than any map.
flavor: You speak the words. The skulls seem lighter afterward. Unburdened.
The rite echoes through the ossuary. For a moment, silence. Real silence.

loot: The arch's keystone is a pelvis bone. Wedged inside it — a small bundle.
Offerings hang from the ribs like ornaments. One is more than decorative.
hazard: You brush a rib and the arch groans. Bones shift and a heavy section drops on you.
The arch is trapped. Touching a specific bone triggers a cascade of falling debris.
intel: The arch frames a specific view — through it, you see markings on the far wall, aligned perfectly.
The bones are arranged by species. The pattern tells a story of what lived here, and what killed it.
secret: The arch is a doorframe. The space beneath it was sealed with packed bone. It can be cleared.
Looking through the arch at the right angle reveals a hidden seam in the floor.
flavor: A cathedral arch made of giant ribs. Whatever this came from was enormous.
The arch frames nothing. A monument to nothing, built from nothing's remains.
You stand beneath the arch and feel small. That was the architect's intention.

loot: You climb the arch like a scaffold. At the apex, hidden from below — a cache.
Your hands find holds others can't see. Inside the arch's structure — a hidden niche.
intel: From the top of the arch, you can see the entire chamber. Passages you missed become obvious.
The arch's construction reveals the builders' routes. You trace them to hidden corridors.
secret: The arch contains a passage within its structure. Narrow, but passable if you're agile.
You find a hidden chamber inside the arch itself. Someone built this to hide in.
flavor: You navigate the bone structure expertly. It contains nothing but more bones.
An impressive climb with a disappointing conclusion. The top of the arch is just... the top.

loot: The whispers guide your hand to a loose bone. Behind it, a small hollow with something inside.
One voice is louder than the rest. It leads you to a specific bone — and what's behind it.
hazard: The whispers crescendo into a shriek. The sound is physical — it hurts.
You listen too long. The voices become words. The words become a headache that draws blood.
intel: The whispers speak of routes and rooms. Not clearly, but enough to piece together.
You catch fragments — names of chambers, warnings about what guards them.
The wall whispers a count. Footsteps in the hall beyond. Something is pacing.
flavor: The wall murmurs in a language you don't know. It sounds like grieving.
You press your ear to the bone. The whispers stop. When you pull away, they resume.
A choir of the dead, singing too quietly to hear. You catch the melody, not the words.

loot: You speak the dead's language. They answer, and they give — a bone clicks out of the wall, revealing a gift.
The communion is profound. The wall offers what it has been holding.
intel: The dead tell you everything. Every room, every danger, whispered directly into your mind.
You commune and the ossuary opens its secrets. The dead have been waiting for someone who listens.
The whispers become clear speech. They warn you. They guide you. They are desperate to help.
flavor: The wall falls silent when you speak. Respectful, perhaps. Or exhausted.
You commune with the dead. They have nothing to say. Sometimes the dead are just dead.

loot: The lid is cracked enough to peer inside. Grave goods — still intact after all this time.
The sarcophagus has already been opened. But the looters missed the false bottom.
hazard: You shift the lid and gas escapes — trapped decay, concentrated and toxic.
The occupant's hand grabs your wrist as the lid moves. Death grip. Literally.
The lid is heavier than expected. It slides and catches your fingers beneath it.
intel: The carvings on the lid depict the ossuary's layout. A burial map, carved in bone.
Inscriptions name the occupant — and their role. A guardian. Of what?
flavor: The sarcophagus is sealed. Whatever's inside has been resting a long time. You let it rest.
You trace the carvings. A life, told in bone relief. Birth, war, death. The universal story.
The stone is cold. The kind of cold that suggests the occupant is still home.

loot: The lid grinds aside. Grave goods — weapon, armour, offerings. The dead were buried rich.
You force the sarcophagus open. Inside, alongside the bones, something worth the desecration.
The false bottom gives way to brute force. The real treasure was beneath the body.
hazard: The lid comes free and the occupant sits up. Not alive — but articulated. And angry.
You force it open and the trapped air ignites. Ancient preservation chemicals, still volatile.
The stone lid shatters when forced. Fragments everywhere. Several find you.
flavor: You open the sarcophagus. Bones, dust, and nothing else. Grave robbery with no reward.
The occupant is thoroughly dead and thoroughly looted. Someone got here first.

loot: The cage's occupant is long gone. Their belongings are not.
Something glints through the bone bars. Reachable, if you stretch.
hazard: The cage swings when you touch it. The chain snaps. Several kilograms of bone crash down.
You reach through the bars and something inside grabs you. Not bone. Something living.
intel: The cage faces an exit — the prisoner's view for their last days. They scratched a message.
Marks on the cage bars. A countdown. The last number is circled.
flavor: A cage of fused bone, swaying gently. Its occupant became part of the material.
The cage is empty. The chain is not rusted. Someone empties it regularly.
You wonder who builds a cage from bone. Then you wonder who fills it. Then you stop wondering.

loot: You find the false bone in the cage floor. It's hollow — a hiding spot for the prisoner's valuables.
Your hands find a seam the cage builders missed. A hidden compartment in the frame.
intel: The cage's occupant left a message in the pattern of scratches. You read it like braille.
Hidden scratches on the inner bars — a map of the ossuary, drawn by someone with nothing but time.
secret: The cage was placed here to draw attention from a passage behind it. You find it.
A section of the cage opens like a door. It leads to a narrow crawlway in the wall.
flavor: You search every bone of the cage. Nothing. Just a well-built prison.
The cage holds nothing but dust and the memory of suffering.

loot: Something solid rests at the bottom of the viscous pool. You fish it out, gagging.
The pool has preserved an object perfectly. You extract it with considerable reluctance.
hazard: The pool is not marrow. Or not just marrow. It burns where it touches skin.
You reach in and something under the surface bites. The pool is inhabited.
The fumes from the pool hit you without warning. Your vision swims.
flavor: A pool of rendered bone marrow. The smell is indescribable. You try not to describe it.
Thick, yellowish, and warm. The pool bubbles gently. You do not investigate why.
The ossuary renders its own. This pool is where the bones end up. Eventually.

loot: Analysis reveals a concentration of minerals at the pool's centre. Extractable and valuable.
The marrow has preserved something in suspension. Your careful extraction recovers it intact.
intel: The composition tells you what died here and when. The bones are recent. The danger is current.
Chemical analysis of the pool reveals it's fed from multiple chambers. You map the connections.
The marrow contains traces from species in adjacent rooms. You know what's nearby.
flavor: Thorough chemical analysis of bone marrow. Conclusion: it's bone marrow. Truly groundbreaking.
You study the pool with professional detachment. Your stomach studies it with professional revulsion.

loot: The obsidian has flowed around an object, encasing it. You can still reach it from one side.
The glass surface is smooth enough to reflect. In the reflection, you spot something embedded.
hazard: Volcanic glass is sharp. You find out by kneeling on a thin edge.
The flow is still warm. Not hot — until you press your palm flat against it.
A thin section shatters when you lean on it. Glass shards and a cut hand.
intel: The flow pattern shows where magma channels run beneath the floor. Useful routing information.
The obsidian captured gas bubbles. Their orientation tells you about the cave's ventilation.
flavor: Black glass, perfectly smooth, reflecting firelight. The mountain's blood, cooled and hardened.
You see yourself in the obsidian. The heat has not been kind to your appearance.
The flow is ancient. The mountain exhaled here once, long ago, and the breath froze.

loot: You crack the obsidian like an egg. Inside, preserved by the glass — something valuable.
The flow shatters under force. Embedded within — an artifact, sealed by the eruption.
hazard: The obsidian explodes outward when struck. Glass shrapnel at velocity.
You break it open and heat pours out. The interior is still hot, even after centuries.
flavor: You smash beautiful obsidian into ugly rubble. Nothing inside but more glass.
Effective destruction. Zero discovery. The obsidian was solid all the way through.

loot: The tube's interior has mineral deposits. Among them, something that was carried here by the flow.
A previous explorer cached supplies inside the tube opening. Still there.
hazard: The tube exhales hot gas without warning. You inhale before you can stop yourself.
The tube walls are scalding. You touch before you test. Classic.
Loose rock inside the tube shifts and falls. In the enclosed space, there's nowhere to dodge.
intel: The tube extends beyond this room. Following it would reveal the cave's volcanic plumbing.
Air flows through the tube from a distant source. There are open chambers connected to this.
The tube is part of a network. You can hear echoes of other rooms through it.
secret: The tube is passable. Tight, hot, but it leads somewhere not on any map.
The tube opens into darkness. Not a dead end — a passage the mountain built.
flavor: A tunnel carved by liquid rock. The walls are glass-smooth and still warm.
You peer into the tube. Heat shimmers. The darkness wavers. You decide not to crawl in.
The mountain's old veins. Once, fire flowed here. Now just heat and memory.

loot: You send a probe into the tube network. It returns with a mineral sample — and something else.
Mapping the tube reveals a junction with a collapsed section. Behind the debris — a sealed cache.
intel: The tube network maps the volcanic plumbing. You can predict where the floor is safe and where it isn't.
Acoustic mapping through the tube reveals the full chamber layout. Your mental map expands dramatically.
Temperature gradients through the tube tell you where active magma flows. Avoid those rooms.
secret: The tube connects to a chamber not visible from any passage. You can reach it.
Your mapping reveals a junction the builders couldn't have known about. A natural shortcut.
flavor: You map the tube network in detail. It's a dead end. A very well-mapped dead end.
Thorough surveying confirms: hot tube, goes nowhere useful. Science.

loot: The slag contains metal — refined by the volcano's heat. Some of it is useful.
You sift through the slag and find a worked object. Someone was forging down here.
hazard: The slag is still hot in places. Your hand finds one of those places.
Sharp edges hidden in the loose material. You cut yourself before you see the blood.
The heap shifts. Volcanic debris cascades over your feet. It's heavier than it looks.
flavor: Volcanic waste, cooled into ugly lumps. The mountain's garbage.
You kick through the slag. Clinkers and pumice and worthless rock.
The heap radiates warmth. The mountain is always working, even in its waste.

loot: Aggressive digging uncovers a sealed container, heat-treated and intact.
At the bottom of the heap — a forge-sealed box. Whatever's inside survived the eruption.
hazard: You dig and the slag collapses inward. You're waist-deep in hot rubble.
The heap conceals a vent. You dig into it and superheated gas jets out.
Your enthusiasm exceeds your caution. Burns on both hands. Worth it? No.
flavor: You dig through the entire heap. Slag, all the way down. Just... slag.
Thorough excavation reveals nothing. The mountain produced junk and you sorted it.

loot: The vent's rim has mineral deposits. One crystallised formation is worth extracting.
Something was dropped near the vent — too hot to retrieve carelessly, but you manage.
hazard: The vent belches steam without warning. Second-degree burns, instantly.
You lean over the vent. It erupts. Your eyebrows are gone and your face is scalded.
The ground around the vent is thin. Your foot breaks through into hot rock.
intel: The vent's output tells you about magma flows below. You can predict the hot zones.
Steam patterns indicate connected chambers. The vent network maps the volcanic system.
flavor: The vent breathes hot air steadily. The mountain's exhalation, endless and indifferent.
You hold your hand over the vent. Warm, then hot, then you pull away. The mountain doesn't care.
Sulfurous steam, yellow-tinged. The smell of the earth's interior. Rotten eggs and apocalypse.

loot: Gas composition analysis reveals rare mineral deposits in the vent shaft. You extract a sample.
The vent cycles predictably. During a lull, you reach in and recover a slag-encrusted artifact.
intel: The gas composition maps the volcanic system. You know where the magma is active — and where it isn't.
Temperature cycling tells you about chamber sizes nearby. Large rooms cool the output measurably.
You predict the vent's eruption cycle. Twelve seconds safe, three seconds deadly. Now you know.
flavor: Detailed analysis of volcanic gases. Conclusion: the mountain is active. You needed a scientist for this.
You study the emissions thoroughly. The vent continues to vent, indifferent to your expertise.

loot: The flow's surface is cracked. In a gap — something that was carried and then entombed.
The lava flowed over an old campsite. Some of the gear was sealed inside, protected by the rock.
hazard: The crust is thin here. Your foot breaks through into still-warm rock.
You walk on the flow and it shifts. Not solid — just crusted over something molten.
intel: The flow's direction tells you where the magma came from. The source chamber is close.
The lava followed existing passages. Its path maps routes you can't see.
flavor: Frozen waves of stone, mid-flow. The moment the mountain's fury stopped.
The flow is beautiful in its violence. Destruction, preserved as art.
You walk on cooled lava. Beneath your feet, the record of something catastrophic.

loot: Your blessing cools the stone further. Cracks widen and release what was trapped inside.
The consecrated flow surrenders its contents. The mountain's anger gives way to generosity.
intel: Through the consecration, you feel the mountain's pain. It shows you where it hurts — the active zones.
The flow carries memory. Your blessing unlocks it — a vision of the volcanic system.
secret: The flow covered a passage. Your consecration reveals the seam — it can be cleared.
Beneath the cooled lava, something the mountain tried to hide. A passage, sealed by eruption.
flavor: You bless the stone. The heat fades slightly. A small mercy in a merciless place.
The consecration holds. The flow is peaceful now, whatever violence created it.

loot: The embers glow around a dark object. Not coal — something manufactured, heat-treated.
Among the embers, a crystal that thrives in fire. It's warm but intact.
hazard: You reach for an ember and it flares. Not dying — dormant. The burst of heat is vicious.
The cluster looks dead. It isn't. Your hand disagrees with your assessment.
Embers scatter when disturbed. One lands on your shoulder and burns through cloth.
flavor: Glowing coals that never cool. The mountain's pilot light, always ready.
The embers pulse like a heartbeat. The mountain is alive, and you're inside it.
You warm your hands over the embers. The first comfortable moment since you descended. Brief.

loot: The forge's quenching trough still holds tools. One is salvageable.
Half-finished work sits on the anvil. Abandoned mid-strike. Still useful.
The forge's fuel bin contains more than coal. Someone stored valuables among the cinders.
hazard: The forge still has live coals. You discover this by reaching in.
You bump the anvil and the half-finished work rolls off. It's hot. Onto your foot.
intel: The forge was producing weapons. The designs are unfamiliar — whoever built here wasn't human.
Work orders scratched into the anvil's base. Lists of chambers, quantities, deadlines.
flavor: A forge built into the living rock, powered by the mountain itself. Abandoned but not broken.
Someone worked metal here, using the volcano as their furnace. The ambition is staggering.
The anvil rings when you touch it. A clear, deep note. The forge remembers its purpose.

loot: You understand the forge's design. The waste bin contains failed castings — one is almost perfect.
The quenching technique is unique. You replicate it and recover a tempered piece from the trough.
intel: The forge's design reveals the builder's sophistication. You can predict what else they built — and where.
Material analysis of the forge slag tells you what was produced here. Weapons, locks, mechanisms.
The forge's ventilation system connects to the broader cave network. You map the airflow.
flavor: You appreciate the engineering enormously. The forge doesn't reciprocate.
A masterwork of volcanic forge design. You understand every component. None of them help you.
